name: Biscotti Di Prato
recipeInstructions:
This recipe makes about 4 dozen very crunchy biscotti. I've never put chocolate on the biscotti.
You need two 12x15 inch (approx.) cookie sheets. Butter and flour one of the cookie sheets. The oven should be set to 350°F for roasting almonds, and 300°F for baking biscotti. Total baking time for the biscotti is about 2 hours. You also need a surface for kneading the biscotti dough and another for cutting the baked biscotti.
Roast the almonds in the preheated oven at 350°F until lightly browned (10-15 minutes). Allow nuts to cool completely. Lower the oven temperature to 300°F. In a medium bowl, mix together the dry ingredients (flour, sugar, baking soda, salt) just to combine. Separate about 1/2 cup of this mixture and spread some onto your kneading surface. In a small bowl or measuring cup, lightly beat together the eggs and vanilla. Make a well in the flour mixture and pour in the egg/vanilla mixture. Stir from the outside of the well into the flour until the mixture is stiff enough to not stir easily. Turn the mixture out onto your floured kneading surface. With floured hands (using more reserved dry ingredients if needed), fold the dough over itself until it coheres. Use the remaining reserved dry ingredients for your hands and work surface, as necessary. Press the nuts into the dough and continue folding the dough until the nuts are evenly distributed. Allow the dough to rest a few minutes so the flour absorbs any liquid, making the dough less sticky. Divide the dough into 3 equal pieces. Roll each piece with your hands into elongated strips about 1 inch wide and 12-14 inches long.
Place the strips on the buttered/floured baking sheet, leaving about 4 inches between each strip. If desired, flatten the strips to a width of 2 inches. Bake for about 50 minutes at 300°F. Allow the strips to cool for 5 minutes before removing them with a spatula onto a cutting surface. Using a sharp knife, make downward strokes to cut the strips into diagonal bars about 1/2 inch wide.
Lay the cut biscotti on their sides on the two baking sheets. Toast for 35-50 minutes at 300°F, depending on how dark and crunchy you prefer them.
